description: If it is, then it ain't what it ain't.

hints.text: The goal is $A \to \lnot \lnot A$. If $A$ is true, then its [double-negation|double negation] should also be true. To prove $\lnot \lnot A$, you'll need `not-intro` with $\lnot A$ as the [formula|formula] to negate. Start by assuming $A$.

Now you need to prove $\lnot \lnot A$ under the [assumption|assumption] $A$. The rule `not-intro (not A) N M` requires two subproofs that share $\lnot A$ in their [context|context] and reach contradictory [conclusion|conclusions]. To get $\lnot A$ into a context, assume it.
Now you have $A$ at step 1 and $\lnot A$ at step 2, but in separate contexts. Use `and-intro` to merge them — the union of the two contexts will contain both.
The merged [context|context] now has both $A$ and $\lnot A$. Extract each one individually — they'll share the same context. First, pull out $A$ with `and-elim-l`.
Steps 4 and 5 both have $\lnot A$ in their contexts, and prove $A$ and $\lnot A$ — a [contradiction|contradiction]. Apply `not-intro` to get $\lnot \lnot A$.
Discharge the original [assumption|assumption] $A$ to get the full [implication|implication].
